title: API stability policy
text: PESTO follows published two-phase policy. phase boundary 1.0.0 release.

title: API stability policy
text: Versions 0.x.y follow additive--intent evolution. Every minor release (0.x → 0.(x+1)) adds new exports without breaking existing signatures; patch releases fix bugs refresh documentation. Track record far: 0.1.0 — first release (core IES / GLM solvers, SVD backends, surrogates, PEST++ integration, visualisation). 0.1.1 — sole-authorship consolidation; API change. 0.2.0 — added pesto_ies_callback() (-process IES driver) apsim_callback() (apsimx adapter). breaking changes. 0.3.0 — added pesto_ensemble_manifest (S7 cross-package contract) plus as_manifest(), write_manifest(), read_manifest(), verify_manifest(). breaking changes. 0.3.1 — added format = c("rds","","csv") write_manifest(); backwards-compatible YAML. 0.3.2 — renamed format = "csv" "csv_unverified" every call site; legacy "csv" still accepted deprecation warning, 0.3.1 manifests round-trip cleanly. 0.3.3 — Makevars FLIBS portability fix; API change. policy “additive--intent” rather “frozen”: pre-1.0 reserves right break existing signature design flaw surfaces, every change must : Listed ## Breaking changes heading NEWS.md, first. Justified release notes. feasible, accompanied temporary back-compat shim (v0.3.2 csv → csv_unverified rename template: legacy spelling still works deprecation warning). depend PESTO pre-1.0, pin version renv.lock DESCRIPTION (Imports: PESTO (>= 0.3.3)).

title: API stability policy
text: 1.0.0, PESTO adopts strict frozen-API additive evolution — policy glmnet, mgcv, long-lived solver-style packages: Signatures exported functions never change backwards-incompatible way across major versions. New capability arrives via new entry points (new exports), never via changes existing ones. existing function genuinely needs retired, marked lifecycle::deprecate_warn(), kept ≥ 2 minor versions, promoted lifecycle::deprecate_stop() ≥ 1 minor version, removed next major release. Successors signposted deprecation message. policy chosen PESTO invoked IES / surrogate backend pipeline code maintainer edit — kernR consumes pesto_ensemble_manifest S7 contract; APSIM ensemble loops via pesto_ies_callback() written run life calibration campaign. Silent breakage across versions defeat contract.

Build-time defects PESTO’s sources compromise installed artefacts. scope: Defects upstream dependencies (Rcpp, Eigen, R ). Report relevant upstream project; coordinate PESTO usage entry point. title: In-Process IES via R Callback -- apsimx Adapter
text: pesto_ies() shells pestpp-ies binary, writes reads .pst / ensemble / observation files every realisation. -process R forward model (apsimx wrapper, Python bridge, fast synthetic test problem), file round-trip pure overhead. pesto_ies_callback() keeps C++ ensemble kernel (ensemble_solution(), Chen & Oliver 2013) drives outer loop R, calling forward model -process. document shows: driver synthetic linear-Gaussian problem (recovers truth). shape apsim_callback() adapter apsimx (run block disabled – needs APSIM installed).

title: In-Process IES via R Callback -- apsimx Adapter
text: Run six IES iterations single lambda: Diagnostics: Posterior RMSE small fraction prior RMSE, mean phi decrease across iterations. full per-realisation phi trajectory:
code: library(PESTO)  npar  <- 4L nobs  <- 8L nreal <- 120L sigma <- 0.05  G          <- matrix(rnorm(nobs * npar), nobs, npar) theta_true <- c(1.0, -0.5, 2.0, 0.25) y          <- as.numeric(G %*% theta_true) + rnorm(nobs, sd = sigma) names(y)   <- paste0("o", seq_len(nobs))  # Forward model: any function that takes an (nreal x npar) matrix and # returns an (nreal x nobs) matrix. forward <- function(theta) theta %*% t(G)  prior <- matrix(rnorm(nreal * npar), nreal, npar,                 dimnames = list(NULL, paste0("p", seq_len(npar)))) fit <- pesto_ies_callback(   forward_model  = forward,   prior_ensemble = prior,   obs            = y,   obs_sd         = sigma,   noptmax        = 6L,   lambda         = 1.0,   verbose        = FALSE ) prior_rmse <- sqrt(mean((colMeans(prior) - theta_true)^2)) post_mean  <- colMeans(as.matrix(fit$par_ensemble[, -1L])) post_rmse  <- sqrt(mean((post_mean - theta_true)^2))  cat(sprintf("prior RMSE: %.4f\n", prior_rmse)) #> prior RMSE: 1.1300 cat(sprintf("posterior RMSE: %.4f\n", post_rmse)) #> posterior RMSE: 0.0232  mean_phi <- vapply(fit$iterations, `[[`, numeric(1L), "mean_phi") print(mean_phi) #> [1] 17452.123014     1.534500     1.533925     1.533362     1.532812 #> [6]     1.532273 plot_phi(fit) #> Warning in melt.data.table(phi_dt, id.vars = iter_col, measure.vars = phi_cols, #> : 'measure.vars' [realisation, phi] are not all of the same type. By order of #> hierarchy, the molten data value column will be of type 'double'. All measure #> variables not of type 'double' will be coerced too. Check DETAILS in #> ?melt.data.table for more on coercion.

title: In-Process IES via R Callback -- apsimx Adapter
text: adapter wraps apsimx realisation gets working copy APSIM template, parameter edits per param_map, run, extraction step. signature returned matches pesto_ies_callback() expects.
code: library(apsimx)   # >= 2.7.0 from CRAN # Requires a working APSIM Next Gen installation.  forward <- apsim_callback(   template  = "wheat_wagga.apsimx",   param_map = list(     RUE = "Wheat.Leaf.Photosynthesis.RUE.FixedValue",     CN2 = "Soil.SoilWater.CN2Bare"   ),   output_extractor = function(sim) {     # sim is the data.frame of report-table variables.     as.numeric(sim$Wheat.Grain.Total.Wt)   } )  prior <- cbind(   RUE = runif(40, 1.0, 2.0),   CN2 = runif(40, 60,  90) )  fit <- pesto_ies_callback(   forward_model  = forward,   prior_ensemble = prior,   obs            = c(y_2018 = 4500, y_2019 = 5200, y_2020 = 4900),   obs_sd         = 250,   noptmax        = 4L )  summary(fit$par_ensemble)

title: In-Process IES via R Callback -- apsimx Adapter
text: Per-realisation APSIM crashes (corrupt config, solver divergence, missing report variable) caught adapter emerge NA rows. pesto_ies_callback()’s on_failure = "na" (default) carries realisations forward unchanged ensemble survives partial failures. Setting on_failure = "stop" aborts soon one row missing.

title: In-Process IES via R Callback -- apsimx Adapter
text: Phase-1 D4 runs realisations serially. Parallel evaluation (future, mirai, apsimx’s job-server modes) planned follow-apsimx’s thread-safety ensemble load characterised. pure-R synthetic models serial loop already fast enough overhead dominated ensemble_solution() .

title: In-Process IES via R Callback -- apsimx Adapter
text: Use classic .pst path : forward model lives behind non-R executable PEST++ can drive directly via template/instruction-file mechanism. need full bit--bit compatibility pestpp-ies behaviour (e.g. cross-validating existing PEST++ workflow). depend pestpp-ies-specific features yet exposed R driver (full lambda line-search, regularisation modes, Tikhonov priors). Otherwise callback driver faster iterate, easier debug, avoids per-realisation file /O entirely.

title: Ensemble Manifests -- the Cross-Package Contract
text: PESTO ensemble run produces moderately large pile state: parameter ensemble, simulated outputs, target observations, IES weights, RNG seed, lambda schedule, runtime context. let kernR, proxymix, paper-writing pipeline consume output without reaching PESTO-specific list internals, v0.3.0 introduces versioned S7 contract object – pesto_ensemble_manifest – plus YAML+CSV persistence SHA-256 integrity checking. Year-1 §A5 UQ ag-stack roadmap. deliberately infrastructure rather methodology: value every downstream consumer reads object, provenance guarantees, every run.

what: Constructing a manifest from an IES run
title: Ensemble Manifests -- the Cross-Package Contract
text: Wrap result: Slots reachable via standard S7 @ accessor:
code: library(PESTO)  set.seed(20260516) npar <- 3L; nobs <- 6L; nreal <- 60L  G <- matrix(rnorm(nobs * npar), nobs, npar) theta_true <- c(1.0, -0.5, 2.0) y <- as.numeric(G %*% theta_true) + rnorm(nobs, sd = 0.05) names(y) <- paste0("o", seq_len(nobs))  prior <- matrix(rnorm(nreal * npar), nreal, npar,                 dimnames = list(NULL, paste0("p", seq_len(npar))))  fit <- pesto_ies_callback(   forward_model  = function(theta) theta %*% t(G),   prior_ensemble = prior,   obs            = y,   obs_sd         = 0.05,   noptmax        = 4L,   verbose        = FALSE ) m <- as_manifest(fit, seed = 20260516L,                  apsim_version = NA_character_) print(m) #> <pesto_ensemble_manifest> schema 1.0.0 #>   run_id        : ies_callback_20260530_031616_d187d01c #>   method        : ies_callback  (noptmax=4) #>   ensemble      : 60 realisations x 3 parameters | 6 observations #>   failure rate  : 0.00% #>   pesto version : 0.4.1  apsim: NA #>   timestamp     : 2026-05-30T03:16:16+0000 #>   data hash     : sha256:e7b630ad06429b528fa6a57a4973894eb9bf2709a6a3ffeb01366ede48b78ed6 m@run_id #> [1] "ies_callback_20260530_031616_d187d01c" m@data_hash #> [1] "sha256:e7b630ad06429b528fa6a57a4973894eb9bf2709a6a3ffeb01366ede48b78ed6" m@noptmax #> [1] 4 head(m@params) #>   real_name       p1         p2       p3 #> 1    real_1 1.029000 -0.4654747 2.011874 #> 2    real_2 1.026500 -0.4658594 2.013726 #> 3    real_3 1.032378 -0.4622419 2.014441 #> 4    real_4 1.031622 -0.4617477 2.015089 #> 5    real_5 1.034491 -0.4633193 2.014088 #> 6    real_6 1.034192 -0.4611789 2.017081

title: Ensemble Manifests -- the Cross-Package Contract
text: write_manifest() emits YAML plus three RDS sidecars (*_params.rds, *_outputs.rds, *_assim.rds). RDS used preference CSV IEEE 754 doubles round-trip bit-exactly – SHA-256 integrity check otherwise trip formatter precision loss: Read back confirm integrity hash matches: peek YAML actually looks like (truncated):
code: dir <- tempfile("pesto_manifest_") dir.create(dir) paths <- write_manifest(m, file.path(dir, "wagga_2026_run01.yaml")) basename(paths) #> [1] "wagga_2026_run01.yaml"        "wagga_2026_run01_params.rds"  #> [3] "wagga_2026_run01_outputs.rds" "wagga_2026_run01_assim.rds" m2 <- read_manifest(file.path(dir, "wagga_2026_run01.yaml")) verify_manifest(m2)$ok #> [1] TRUE cat(paste(readLines(file.path(dir, "wagga_2026_run01.yaml"))[1:14],           collapse = "\n")) #> schema_version: 1.0.0 #> run_id: ies_callback_20260530_031616_d187d01c #> data_hash: sha256:e7b630ad06429b528fa6a57a4973894eb9bf2709a6a3ffeb01366ede48b78ed6 #> format: rds #> integrity: verifiable #> artefacts: #>   params: wagga_2026_run01_params.rds #>   outputs: wagga_2026_run01_outputs.rds #>   assim: wagga_2026_run01_assim.rds #> seed: 20260516 #> fidelity: ~ #> apsim_version: ~ #> pesto_version: 0.4.1 #> timestamp: 2026-05-30T03:16:16+0000

title: Ensemble Manifests -- the Cross-Package Contract
text: workflows need human-readable view ensemble – quick scans Excel, ad-hoc exports domain collaborator, copy-paste Quarto narrative – write_manifest() accepts format = "" argument emits CSV sidecars alongside RDS. hash stays bound RDS (bit-exact integrity preserved), YAML records inspection paths separate inspection_csv: block consumers can ignore : format = "csv_unverified" mode (renamed "csv" PESTO 0.3.2) available one-way exports non-R analysts round-trip integrity required. YAML carries integrity: not_verifiable downstream tools can branch weaker contract. verify_manifest() function returns ok = NA rather spurious FALSE CSV formatter precision loss (~1 ULP IEEE 754 epsilon) enough flip hash: need human inspection verifiable integrity, use format = "". csv_unverified mode deliberately named flag weaker contract every call-site. export, storage intend re-load trust.
code: dir2 <- tempfile("pesto_manifest_csv_"); dir.create(dir2) paths2 <- write_manifest(m, file.path(dir2, "wagga_2026_run01.yaml"),                          format = "both") basename(paths2) #> [1] "wagga_2026_run01.yaml"                   #> [2] "wagga_2026_run01_params.rds"             #> [3] "wagga_2026_run01_outputs.rds"            #> [4] "wagga_2026_run01_assim.rds"              #> [5] "wagga_2026_run01_params_inspection.csv"  #> [6] "wagga_2026_run01_outputs_inspection.csv" #> [7] "wagga_2026_run01_assim_inspection.csv" dir3 <- tempfile("pesto_manifest_unverified_"); dir.create(dir3) write_manifest(m, file.path(dir3, "snapshot.yaml"),                format = "csv_unverified") m_csv <- read_manifest(file.path(dir3, "snapshot.yaml")) verify_manifest(m_csv)$ok      # NA -- see $message for why #> [1] NA

title: Ensemble Manifests -- the Cross-Package Contract
text: downstream tool silently re-saves outputs sidecar (accidental edit, partial overwrite) hash match: verify_manifest() returns stored vs recomputed hashes downstream consumer can fail fast report divergence cleanly.
code: out_rds <- file.path(dir, "wagga_2026_run01_outputs.rds") df <- readRDS(out_rds) df[1, 2] <- df[1, 2] + 1e-3       # perturb one cell saveRDS(df, out_rds, version = 3L)  m3 <- read_manifest(file.path(dir, "wagga_2026_run01.yaml")) v  <- verify_manifest(m3) v$ok #> [1] FALSE

title: Ensemble Manifests -- the Cross-Package Contract
text: manifest single object kernR (HSIC identifiability, DR-DATE counterfactuals, MMD posterior-predictive checks) proxymix (GMM density-ratio bridges) consume. dispatching S7 class, packages never see PESTO-internal list shapes. read m@params, m@outputs, m@weights, m@obs_target contract let verify_manifest() gate integrity. companion jstyle outputs_manifest.yaml (project-level artefact index) reference per-run manifests like one relative path + data_hash, project’s manifest tree end--end hash-verifiable.

title: Getting Started with PESTO
text: PESTO (Parameter ESTimation Optimised) R package provides high-performance parameter estimation, uncertainty quantification, inverse modelling capabilities. builds modernised PEST++ algorithms developed USGS, bringing powerful tools natively R ecosystem.

title: Getting Started with PESTO
text: Iterative Ensemble Smoother (IES): Ensemble-based parameter estimation scales 100,000+ parameters without computing explicit Jacobians Gauss-Levenberg-Marquardt (GLM): Deterministic gradient-based estimation SVD regularisation Global Sensitivity Analysis: Morris Sobol methods Parametric Sweep: Embarrassingly parallel model evaluations Publication-quality diagnostics: ggplot2-based visualisations

what: Quick Start: Creating a Model Scenario
title: Getting Started with PESTO
text: Rather requiring pre-existing PEST control file, PESTO lets define problems programmatically:

title: Getting Started with PESTO
text: PESTO provides ensemble_solution() function – high-performance C++ implementation IES update equation. computational heart package:
code: set.seed(42) npar  <- 50 nobs  <- 100 nreal <- 30  # Generate synthetic ensemble differences par_diff  <- matrix(rnorm(npar * nreal, sd = 0.1), npar, nreal) obs_diff  <- matrix(rnorm(nobs * nreal, sd = 1.0), nobs, nreal) obs_resid <- matrix(rnorm(nobs * nreal, sd = 0.5), nobs, nreal) par_resid <- matrix(rnorm(npar * nreal, sd = 0.1), npar, nreal) weights   <- rep(1.0, nobs) parcov_inv <- rep(1.0, npar) Am <- matrix(rnorm(npar * (nreal - 1)), npar, nreal - 1)  # Run the ensemble update upgrade <- ensemble_solution(   par_diff    = par_diff,   obs_diff    = obs_diff,   obs_resid   = obs_resid,   par_resid   = par_resid,   weights     = weights,   parcov_inv  = parcov_inv,   Am          = Am,   cur_lam     = 1.0 )  cat("Upgrade matrix dimensions:", dim(upgrade), "\n") #> Upgrade matrix dimensions: 30 50 cat("Mean absolute upgrade:", mean(abs(upgrade)), "\n") #> Mean absolute upgrade: 0.01283321

title: Getting Started with PESTO
text: PESTO automatically selects fastest SVD algorithm problem. low-rank decompositions (typical IES ensemble size << observations), randomised SVD provides dramatic speedups:
code:
set.seed(42) A <- matrix(rnorm(1000 * 500), 1000, 500)  # Automatic selection res_auto <- adaptive_svd(A, k = 20L, method = "auto") cat("Method:", res_auto$method_used, "
") #> Method: rsvd (Halko-Martinsson-Tropp) cat("Time:", round(res_auto$time_ms, 2), "ms
") #> Time: 18.99 ms cat("Singular values (top 5):", round(res_auto$d[1:5], 3), "
") #> Singular values (top 5): 50.643 50.283 49.956 49.689 49.454  # Compare: randomised SVD (fast, rank-k) vs full res_rsvd <- rsvd(A, k = 20) cat("
rSVD dimensions: U =", dim(res_rsvd$u), ", V =", dim(res_rsvd$v), "
") #>  #> rSVD dimensions: U = 1000 20 , V = 500 20

set.seed(42) npar <- 100; nobs <- 500; nreal <- 50  pd <- matrix(rnorm(npar * nreal), npar, nreal) od <- matrix(rnorm(nobs * nreal), nobs, nreal) or_ <- matrix(rnorm(nobs * nreal), nobs, nreal) pr <- matrix(rnorm(npar * nreal), npar, nreal) w <- rep(1.0, nobs); pc <- rep(1.0, npar) Am <- matrix(rnorm(npar * (nreal - 1)), npar, nreal - 1)  result <- ensemble_solution_gpu(   pd, od, or_, pr, w, pc, Am,   cur_lam = 1.0, svd_method = "auto" )  cat("SVD method:", result$svd_method, "
") #> SVD method: LAPACK (platform-optimised) cat("SVD time:", round(result$svd_time_ms, 2), "ms

title: PEST++ Comparison and Simulation Study
text: vignette three goals: Scenario – show PESTO’s R-native iterative ensemble smoother reproduces results upstream pestpp-ies binary small, well-posed analytical inverse problem (eight parameters, fifteen observations). Scenario B – exercise PESTO higher-dimensional, ill-posed problem (one hundred parameters, two hundred observations) surface wins (randomised SVD speedups, surrogate savings, identifiability diagnostics) honest trade-offs (rank-truncation sensitivity, small-rank rSVD penalties). Scenario C – run fixed-seed Monte-Carlo simulation study exercises every exported PESTO function least reports convergence-rate distributions, surrogate savings, ESS evolution sensitivity-ranking stability. Throughout, comparisons honest. PESTO loses, reported plain numbers, glossed . colourblind-safe palette used everywhere (Wong 2011). Comparison strategy. vignette compares PESTO native IES two reference targets, order CRAN-availability: Pure-R textbook reference – pesto_reference_ies() independent implementation Chen & Oliver (2013) eq. 12 pure R. canonical comparison target shipped package used every reader vignette. Live pestpp-ies binary (developer-) – binary resolvable (via PESTO_PESTPP_BIN environment variable Sys.("pestpp-ies")) developer-side cache present tools/pestpp_benchmark/, vignette extends agreement plot also include upstream binary’s posterior. CRAN check farms see path 1, vignette renders end end external binary dependency.

title: PEST++ Comparison and Simulation Study
text: 1-D exponential-decay response eight unknown parameters 𝛉=(θ1,…,θ8)⊤\boldsymbol\theta = (\theta_1, \ldots, \theta_8)^\top fifteen observations: yi=∑k=18kθke−/10+εi,=1,…,15,εi∼𝒩(0,σ2).y_i \;=\; \sum_{k=1}^{8} k\,\theta_k \, e^{-/ 10}\;+\;\varepsilon_i,    \qquad = 1, \ldots, 15, \quad \varepsilon_i \sim \mathcal{N}(0, \sigma^2). closed-form, analytically tractable inverse problem. parameter weighting k⋅e−/10k \cdot e^{-/10} means high-index parameters poorly identifiable – expect posterior recover first parameters tightly leave tail diffuse. Python forward model (forward.py) invoked PEST++ binary tiny R reimplementation, numerical agreement test rigorous. use bundled ies_10par_xsec benchmark layout pattern, build problem R original benchmark requires external MODFLOW-NWT binary (assumed machine).
code: n_par <- 8L par_names_A <- sprintf("p%d", seq_len(n_par)) theta_true_A <- c(1.20, 0.85, 0.55, 0.40, 0.30, 0.22, 0.16, 0.10)  forward_A <- function(p) {   i <- seq_len(15L)   vapply(i, function(ii) sum(seq_along(p) * p * exp(-ii / 10.0)),          numeric(1)) }  y_true_A <- forward_A(theta_true_A) obs_noise_sd_A <- 0.02 y_obs_A <- y_true_A + rnorm(length(y_true_A), sd = obs_noise_sd_A) weights_A <- rep(1 / obs_noise_sd_A, length(y_obs_A))

what: Build the .pst control file via create_pest_scenario() and write_pst()
title: PEST++ Comparison and Simulation Study
code: parameters_A <- data.table(   parnme    = par_names_A,   partrans  = "log",   parchglim = "factor",   parval1   = 0.5,   parlbnd   = 0.001,   parubnd   = 5.0,   pargp     = "pgrp" ) observations_A <- data.table(   obsnme = sprintf("obs_%02d", seq_len(15)),   obsval = y_obs_A,   weight = weights_A,   obgnme = "obs_g" ) pst_A <- create_pest_scenario(   parameters    = parameters_A,   observations  = observations_A,   model_command = "python3 forward.py",   pestpp_options = list(ies_num_reals = 40L) ) print(pst_A)  scratch_A <- file.path(tempdir(), "pesto_scenA") dir.create(scratch_A, showWarnings = FALSE, recursive = TRUE) write_pst(pst_A, file.path(scratch_A, "scenA.pst")) # Round-trip check pst_A_rt <- read_pst(file.path(scratch_A, "scenA.pst")) stopifnot(pst_A_rt$control_data$npar == n_par)

title: PEST++ Comparison and Simulation Study
text: PESTO’s ensemble_solution() C++ kernel drives iterative ensemble smoother. wrap small R loop (precisely implementation pattern documented ?ensemble_solution).
code: set.seed(20260425L) n_real_A <- 40L n_iter_A <- 6L  # Prior ensemble in log-space, with explicit bounds matching the .pst log_prior_mean <- log(0.5) log_prior_sd   <- 0.6 log_lb <- log(0.001); log_ub <- log(5.0) par_ens_A <- matrix(   rnorm(n_real_A * n_par, mean = log_prior_mean, sd = log_prior_sd),   nrow = n_par, ncol = n_real_A,   dimnames = list(par_names_A, sprintf("r%02d", seq_len(n_real_A))) ) par_ens_A <- pmin(pmax(par_ens_A, log_lb), log_ub)  run_forward_R <- function(par_mat) {   apply(par_mat, 2L, function(lp) forward_A(exp(lp))) } obs_ens_A <- run_forward_R(par_ens_A)  phi_trace_pesto <- numeric(n_iter_A + 1L) phi_trace_pesto[1L] <- mean(compute_phi(   matrix(rep(y_obs_A, n_real_A), nrow = length(y_obs_A)) - obs_ens_A,   weights_A ))  # Lambda schedule mirrors the pestpp-ies Marquardt sweep: try several # damping levels per iteration and accept the smallest-phi candidate. lambda_grid <- c(0.5, 5.0, 50.0)  t0 <- proc.time()["elapsed"] for (iter in seq_len(n_iter_A)) {    par_mean <- rowMeans(par_ens_A)   obs_mean <- rowMeans(obs_ens_A)   par_diff <- par_ens_A - par_mean   obs_diff <- obs_ens_A - obs_mean   # ensemble_solution() expects obs_resid = sim - obs -- required for the GLM   # update's leading negative sign to act as a descent step (see ?ensemble_solution).   obs_resid <- obs_ens_A - matrix(rep(y_obs_A, n_real_A), nrow = length(y_obs_A))   par_resid <- par_diff   Am_iter <- matrix(rnorm(n_par * (n_real_A - 1L)), n_par, n_real_A - 1L)    best_phi <- Inf; best_par <- par_ens_A; best_obs <- obs_ens_A   for (lam in lambda_grid) {     upgrade <- ensemble_solution(       par_diff   = par_diff,       obs_diff   = obs_diff,       obs_resid  = obs_resid,       par_resid  = par_resid,       weights    = weights_A,       parcov_inv = rep(1 / log_prior_sd^2, n_par),       Am         = Am_iter,       cur_lam    = lam,       iter       = as.integer(iter)     )     cand <- pmin(pmax(par_ens_A + t(upgrade), log_lb), log_ub)     cand_obs <- run_forward_R(cand)     cand_phi <- mean(compute_phi(       matrix(rep(y_obs_A, n_real_A), nrow = length(y_obs_A)) - cand_obs,       weights_A     ))     if (cand_phi < best_phi) {       best_phi <- cand_phi; best_par <- cand; best_obs <- cand_obs     }   }   par_ens_A <- best_par   obs_ens_A <- best_obs   phi_trace_pesto[iter + 1L] <- best_phi } runtime_pesto_A <- as.numeric(proc.time()["elapsed"] - t0)  posterior_pesto_A <- exp(t(par_ens_A)) post_mean_pesto <- colMeans(posterior_pesto_A) post_q05_pesto  <- apply(posterior_pesto_A, 2L, quantile, 0.05) post_q95_pesto  <- apply(posterior_pesto_A, 2L, quantile, 0.95) rmse_pesto_A    <- sqrt(mean((post_mean_pesto - theta_true_A)^2))  cat(sprintf("PESTO native IES: %d iterations in %.2fs;  posterior RMSE = %.4f\n",             n_iter_A, runtime_pesto_A, rmse_pesto_A)) #> PESTO native IES: 6 iterations in 0.06s;  posterior RMSE = 0.3548

title: PEST++ Comparison and Simulation Study
text: package ships compact reference cache produced pesto_reference_ies() Scenario problem. every reader vignette sees default. developer-side pestpp-ies cache present tools/pestpp_benchmark/scenario_a_pestpp_ies.rds, replace reference comparator live binary’s posterior. cache produced running pestpp-ies (version 5.2.25) .pst control file noptmax = 6 ies_num_reals = 40. regenerate interactively binary present:
code: cache_path <- system.file("extdata", "pestpp_cache",                           "scenario_a_reference.rds", package = "PESTO") if (!nzchar(cache_path)) {   src_guess <- file.path("..", "inst", "extdata", "pestpp_cache",                          "scenario_a_reference.rds")   if (file.exists(src_guess)) cache_path <- normalizePath(src_guess) } stopifnot(nzchar(cache_path), file.exists(cache_path)) ies_cache <- readRDS(cache_path)  # The reference cache pins to the same y_obs / theta_true as Scenario A. stopifnot(max(abs(ies_cache$y_obs - y_obs_A)) < 1e-10)  posterior_pestpp_A <- as.matrix(   ies_cache$posterior_par[, ies_cache$par_names, with = FALSE] ) post_mean_pestpp <- colMeans(posterior_pestpp_A) post_q05_pestpp  <- apply(posterior_pestpp_A, 2L, quantile, 0.05) post_q95_pestpp  <- apply(posterior_pestpp_A, 2L, quantile, 0.95) rmse_pestpp_A    <- sqrt(mean((post_mean_pestpp - theta_true_A)^2)) runtime_pestpp_A <- NA_real_  phi_actual <- ies_cache$phi_history phi_trace_pestpp <- phi_actual$mean  reference_label <- "pure-R reference (Chen & Oliver 2013)" dev_cache <- file.path("..", "tools", "pestpp_benchmark",                        "scenario_a_pestpp_ies.rds") if (pestpp_available && file.exists(dev_cache)) {   ies_cache <- readRDS(dev_cache)   posterior_pestpp_A <- as.matrix(     ies_cache$posterior_par[, ies_cache$par_names, with = FALSE]   )   post_mean_pestpp <- colMeans(posterior_pestpp_A)   post_q05_pestpp  <- apply(posterior_pestpp_A, 2L, quantile, 0.05)   post_q95_pestpp  <- apply(posterior_pestpp_A, 2L, quantile, 0.95)   rmse_pestpp_A    <- sqrt(mean((post_mean_pestpp - theta_true_A)^2))   runtime_pestpp_A <- ies_cache$runtime_s %||% NA_real_   phi_trace_pestpp <- ies_cache$phi_history$mean   reference_label <- "pestpp-ies binary" } `%||%` <- function(a, b) if (!is.null(a)) a else b if (interactive() && pestpp_available) {   message("Live pestpp-ies run takes ~30s; the vignette uses the cache.") }

title: PEST++ Comparison and Simulation Study
text: 2-D log-transmissivity field log⁡T(x,y)\log T(x,y) 10$$10 grid (one hundred unknowns), feeding steady-state confined-flow finite-difference solver. Heads observed twenty-five well locations discharges five vertical-control sections, giving two hundred observations add temporal snapshots. canonical highly-parameterised -determined setup motivated SVD regularisation. use synthetic R groundwater model rather MODFLOW mf6 binary machine – structure (sparse linear solve, distributed log-transmissivity field) faithful 2-D confined aquifer.
code: have_matrix <- requireNamespace("Matrix", quietly = TRUE) if (!have_matrix) {   message("Matrix not installed; using a base-R dense solver fallback.") }  nx <- 10L; ny <- 10L n_par_B <- nx * ny n_obs_B <- 200L  forward_B <- function(logT_vec) {   # Clip log-transmissivity to a safe range so prior draws never produce   # singular linear systems.   logT_vec <- pmin(pmax(logT_vec, -2.5), 2.5)   Tcell <- exp(logT_vec)                       # cell transmissivities   T <- matrix(Tcell, nx, ny)   # Five-point stencil with harmonic-mean cell-face conductance,   # Dirichlet h = 10 along left edge, h = 0 along right edge.   N <- nx * ny   rows <- integer(0); cols <- integer(0); vals <- numeric(0); rhs <- numeric(N)   idx <- function(i, j) (j - 1L) * nx + i    for (j in seq_len(ny)) {     for (i in seq_len(nx)) {       k <- idx(i, j)       if (i == 1L) {         rows <- c(rows, k); cols <- c(cols, k); vals <- c(vals, 1.0)         rhs[k] <- 10.0       } else if (i == nx) {         rows <- c(rows, k); cols <- c(cols, k); vals <- c(vals, 1.0)         rhs[k] <- 0.0       } else {         diag_val <- 0.0         for (off in list(c(-1L, 0L), c(1L, 0L), c(0L, -1L), c(0L, 1L))) {           ni <- i + off[1L]; nj <- j + off[2L]           if (ni >= 1L && ni <= nx && nj >= 1L && nj <= ny) {             Tface <- 2.0 * T[i, j] * T[ni, nj] / (T[i, j] + T[ni, nj])             rows <- c(rows, k); cols <- c(cols, idx(ni, nj))             vals <- c(vals, -Tface)             diag_val <- diag_val + Tface           }         }         rows <- c(rows, k); cols <- c(cols, k); vals <- c(vals, diag_val)       }     }   }   if (have_matrix) {     A <- Matrix::sparseMatrix(i = rows, j = cols, x = vals, dims = c(N, N))     h <- tryCatch(as.numeric(Matrix::solve(A, rhs)),                   error = function(e) rep(NA_real_, N))     if (anyNA(h)) {       A_reg <- A + Matrix::Diagonal(N, x = 1e-3 * mean(abs(vals)))       h <- as.numeric(Matrix::solve(A_reg, rhs))     }   } else {     # Dense base-R fallback when Matrix is not installed     A_dense <- matrix(0.0, N, N)     for (k in seq_along(rows)) {       A_dense[rows[k], cols[k]] <- A_dense[rows[k], cols[k]] + vals[k]     }     h <- tryCatch(as.numeric(solve(A_dense, rhs)),                   error = function(e) rep(NA_real_, N))     if (anyNA(h)) {       diag(A_dense) <- diag(A_dense) + 1e-3 * mean(abs(vals))       h <- as.numeric(solve(A_dense, rhs))     }   }    # Observation vector: heads at every interior cell (8x8 = 64), plus   # x-direction face fluxes between every adjacent pair of cells in x   # (9 internal faces x 10 rows = 90), plus y-direction face fluxes   # (10 cols x 9 internal faces = 90). 64 + 90 + 90 = 244, trimmed to 200.   head_idx <- as.vector(outer(seq(2L, nx - 1L), seq(2L, ny - 1L), idx))   head_obs <- h[head_idx]   fx_flux <- numeric(0)   for (j in seq_len(ny)) {     for (i in seq_len(nx - 1L)) {       Tface <- 2.0 * T[i, j] * T[i + 1L, j] / (T[i, j] + T[i + 1L, j])       fx_flux <- c(fx_flux, Tface * (h[idx(i, j)] - h[idx(i + 1L, j)]))     }   }   fy_flux <- numeric(0)   for (j in seq_len(ny - 1L)) {     for (i in seq_len(nx)) {       Tface <- 2.0 * T[i, j] * T[i, j + 1L] / (T[i, j] + T[i, j + 1L])       fy_flux <- c(fy_flux, Tface * (h[idx(i, j)] - h[idx(i, j + 1L)]))     }   }   c(head_obs, fx_flux, fy_flux)[seq_len(n_obs_B)] }  # Truth + observations set.seed(20260425L) xs <- seq_len(nx); ys <- seq_len(ny) truth_field <- outer(xs, ys, function(i, j) {   0.5 * sin(2 * pi * i / nx) * cos(2 * pi * j / ny) + 0.2 * (i / nx) }) logT_true <- as.vector(truth_field) y_true_B <- forward_B(logT_true) obs_noise_sd_B <- 0.05 * sd(y_true_B) y_obs_B <- y_true_B + rnorm(length(y_true_B), sd = obs_noise_sd_B) weights_B <- rep(1 / obs_noise_sd_B, length(y_obs_B)) cat("nobs:", length(y_obs_B), "  npar:", n_par_B,     "  obs SD:", round(obs_noise_sd_B, 4), "\n") #> nobs: 200   npar: 100   obs SD: 0.1199

title: PEST++ Comparison and Simulation Study
code:
set.seed(20260425L) n_real_B <- 60L n_iter_B <- 4L  prior_sd <- 0.4 par_ens_B <- matrix(rnorm(n_real_B * n_par_B, sd = prior_sd),                     nrow = n_par_B, ncol = n_real_B) obs_ens_B <- apply(par_ens_B, 2L, forward_B)  phi_B <- numeric(n_iter_B + 1L) phi_B[1L] <- mean(compute_phi(   matrix(rep(y_obs_B, n_real_B), nrow = length(y_obs_B)) - obs_ens_B,   weights_B))  t0 <- proc.time()["elapsed"] for (iter in seq_len(n_iter_B)) {   par_mean <- rowMeans(par_ens_B); obs_mean <- rowMeans(obs_ens_B)   par_diff <- par_ens_B - par_mean; obs_diff <- obs_ens_B - obs_mean   obs_resid <- obs_ens_B - matrix(rep(y_obs_B, n_real_B),                                   nrow = length(y_obs_B))   par_resid <- par_diff   Am_B <- matrix(rnorm(n_par_B * (n_real_B - 1L)), n_par_B, n_real_B - 1L)    upgrade <- ensemble_solution(     par_diff = par_diff, obs_diff = obs_diff,     obs_resid = obs_resid, par_resid = par_resid,     weights = weights_B,     parcov_inv = rep(1 / prior_sd^2, n_par_B),     Am = Am_B, cur_lam = 5.0,     iter = as.integer(iter), reg_factor = 0.1   )   par_ens_B <- par_ens_B + t(upgrade)   par_ens_B <- pmin(pmax(par_ens_B, -2.5), 2.5)   obs_ens_B <- apply(par_ens_B, 2L, forward_B)   phi_B[iter + 1L] <- mean(compute_phi(     matrix(rep(y_obs_B, n_real_B), nrow = length(y_obs_B)) - obs_ens_B,     weights_B)) } runtime_B <- as.numeric(proc.time()["elapsed"] - t0)  # MDA variant on a snapshot, exercises ensemble_solution_mda obs_err_B <- matrix(rnorm(n_obs_B * n_real_B, sd = obs_noise_sd_B),                     n_obs_B, n_real_B) par_diff_B <- par_ens_B - rowMeans(par_ens_B) obs_diff_B <- obs_ens_B - rowMeans(obs_ens_B) obs_resid_B <- obs_ens_B - matrix(rep(y_obs_B, n_real_B),                                   nrow = length(y_obs_B)) mda_upgrade <- ensemble_solution_mda(   par_diff = par_diff_B, obs_diff = obs_diff_B,   obs_resid = obs_resid_B, obs_err = obs_err_B,   cur_lam = 4.0 ) cat(sprintf("Scenario B: %d iter, %d real, %d par, %d obs in %.2fs
",             n_iter_B, n_real_B, n_par_B, n_obs_B, runtime_B)) #> Scenario B: 4 iter, 60 real, 100 par, 200 obs in 0.70s cat(sprintf("Phi reduction: %.2e -> %.2e  (factor %.1f)
",             phi_B[1L], phi_B[length(phi_B)], phi_B[1L] / phi_B[length(phi_B)])) #> Phi reduction: 1.69e+03 -> 3.51e+02  (factor 4.8) cat(sprintf("MDA upgrade dimensions: %d x %d, mean |upgrade| = %.4f
",             nrow(mda_upgrade), ncol(mda_upgrade), mean(abs(mda_upgrade)))) #> MDA upgrade dimensions: 60 x 100, mean |upgrade| = 0.1090

code: sweep_dt <- rbindlist(lapply(c(1e-8, 1e-6, 1e-4, 1e-3, 1e-2, 5e-2, 1e-1), function(thr) {   par_loc <- par_ens_B   obs_loc <- obs_ens_B   par_mean <- rowMeans(par_loc); obs_mean <- rowMeans(obs_loc)   par_diff <- par_loc - par_mean; obs_diff <- obs_loc - obs_mean   obs_resid <- obs_loc - matrix(rep(y_obs_B, n_real_B),                                 nrow = length(y_obs_B))   par_resid <- par_diff   Am_loc <- matrix(rnorm(n_par_B * (n_real_B - 1L)), n_par_B, n_real_B - 1L)    upg <- ensemble_solution(par_diff, obs_diff, obs_resid, par_resid,                            weights_B, rep(1 / prior_sd^2, n_par_B),                            Am_loc, cur_lam = 5.0,                            eigthresh = thr, iter = 1L)   par_new <- pmin(pmax(par_loc + t(upg), -2.5), 2.5)   obs_new <- apply(par_new, 2L, forward_B)   resid <- obs_new - matrix(rep(y_obs_B, n_real_B),                             nrow = length(y_obs_B))   data.table(eigthresh = thr,              phi_after = mean(compute_phi(resid, weights_B))) })) ggplot(sweep_dt, aes(x = eigthresh, y = phi_after)) +   geom_line(colour = PAL[3], linewidth = 1.1) +   geom_point(colour = PAL[3], size = 3) +   scale_x_log10() + scale_y_log10() +   labs(title = "Scenario B: SVD truncation sensitivity",        x = "Eigenvalue threshold (relative)",        y = expression(Phi ~ "after one update")) +   theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +   theme(plot.title = element_text(face = "bold"))

code: J <- t(vapply(seq_len(n_par_B), function(k) {   eps <- 1e-3   lp_plus <- logT_true; lp_plus[k] <- lp_plus[k] + eps   (forward_B(lp_plus) - y_true_B) / eps }, numeric(n_obs_B))) J <- t(J) * weights_B sv <- svd(J) n_keep <- sum(sv$d / sv$d[1] > 1e-3) V <- sv$v[, seq_len(n_keep), drop = FALSE] ident <- rowSums(V^2)  # Write a compact .jcb-style binary for plot_identifiability() reuse jco_path <- file.path(tempdir(), "scenB.jco") .write_jco_binary <- function(file, mat, rownames_, colnames_) {   con <- file(file, "wb"); on.exit(close(con))   writeBin(-as.integer(nrow(mat)), con, size = 4L)   writeBin( as.integer(ncol(mat)), con, size = 4L)   writeBin(as.numeric(mat), con, size = 8L)   for (nm in rownames_) {     writeBin(as.integer(nchar(nm)), con, size = 4L); writeChar(nm, con, eos = NULL)   }   for (nm in colnames_) {     writeBin(as.integer(nchar(nm)), con, size = 4L); writeChar(nm, con, eos = NULL)   } } par_nm_B <- sprintf("logT_%03d", seq_len(n_par_B)) obs_nm_B <- sprintf("y_%03d",    seq_len(n_obs_B)) .write_jco_binary(jco_path, J, obs_nm_B, par_nm_B) plot_identifiability(jco_file = jco_path,                      n_sv = n_keep,                      title = "Scenario B: parameter identifiability") +   theme(axis.text.y = element_text(size = 6))

text: Honest reporting requires showing rSVD wins loses. dominant axis target rank kk relative min⁡(m,n)\min(m,n), absolute matrix size – small kk random-projection sketch decisively faster, k→min⁡(m,n)k \\min(m,n) dense LAPACK algorithm reclaims lead. rSVD vs LAPACK 400 x 200 matrix k varies. adaptive_svd("auto") returns chosen backend dispatch can audited:

set.seed(20260425L) A_bench <- matrix(rnorm(400 * 200), 400, 200)  if (requireNamespace("microbenchmark", quietly = TRUE)) {   ranks <- c(5L, 20L, 50L, 100L, 180L)   bench_dt <- rbindlist(lapply(ranks, function(kk) {     b <- microbenchmark::microbenchmark(       rSVD   = adaptive_svd(A_bench, k = kk, method = "rsvd"),       LAPACK = adaptive_svd(A_bench, method = "accelerate"),       times = 6L     )     m_r <- median(b$time[b$expr == "rSVD"])   / 1e6     m_l <- median(b$time[b$expr == "LAPACK"]) / 1e6     data.table(rank = kk,                rSVD_ms      = round(m_r, 3),                LAPACK_ms    = round(m_l, 3),                speedup_rSVD = round(m_l / m_r, 2))   }))   knitr::kable(bench_dt,                caption = "rSVD vs LAPACK on a 400 x 200 matrix as k varies.") } auto_res <- adaptive_svd(A_bench, k = 20L, method = "auto") acc_res  <- accelerate_svd(A_bench, thin = TRUE) cat("auto chose:    ", auto_res$method_used,     " in ", round(auto_res$time_ms, 2), "ms
", sep = "") #> auto chose:    rsvd (Halko-Martinsson-Tropp) in 6.46ms cat("LAPACK direct: ", round(length(acc_res$d), 0),     " singular values returned

code: set.seed(20260425L) n_par_C <- 30L n_obs_C <- 60L n_real_C <- 30L n_rep_C  <- 50L  G_C <- matrix(rnorm(n_obs_C * n_par_C, sd = 1 / sqrt(n_par_C)),               n_obs_C, n_par_C) forward_C <- function(theta, alpha = 0.2) {   lin <- as.numeric(G_C %*% theta)   lin + alpha * sin(lin) * exp(-0.05 * abs(lin)) }

code: # write/read ensemble round-trip (write_ensemble + read_ensemble) ens_path <- file.path(tempdir(), "ens_demo.csv") demo_ens <- as.data.table(matrix(rnorm(20 * 5), 20, 5)) setnames(demo_ens, paste0("p", seq_len(5))) write_ensemble(demo_ens, ens_path) back_ens <- read_ensemble(ens_path) stopifnot(nrow(back_ens) == 20L)  # pesto_surrogate_ies wrapper exercise (single-iteration shape check) set.seed(20260425L) pe_demo <- matrix(rnorm(40 * n_par_C), 40, n_par_C) oe_demo <- t(apply(pe_demo, 1L, forward_C)) demo_target <- forward_C(rep(0, n_par_C)) + rnorm(n_obs_C, sd = 0.05) demo_surr <- pesto_surrogate_ies(   par_ensemble = pe_demo, obs_ensemble = oe_demo,   obs_target = demo_target,   weights = rep(1 / 0.05, n_obs_C),   parcov_inv = rep(1.0, n_par_C),   uncertainty_threshold = 0.1 ) print(plot_surrogate_diagnostics(list(demo_surr),                                  title = "Surrogate IES (demo)"))

title: PEST++ Comparison and Simulation Study
text: replicate runs PESTO IES, adaptive-ensemble-size check, surrogate-accelerated update, RFF-based prediction exercise GP, RFF, adaptive-sizing exports.
code: set.seed(20260425L) phi_drop_rates <- numeric(n_rep_C) sur_savings    <- numeric(n_rep_C) ess_seq        <- vector("list", n_rep_C) sens_rank      <- matrix(0L, n_rep_C, n_par_C) adaptive_sizes <- integer(n_rep_C) gpu_times_ms   <- numeric(n_rep_C)  theta_truth_C <- matrix(rnorm(n_par_C * n_rep_C), n_par_C, n_rep_C)  for (r in seq_len(n_rep_C)) {   theta_t <- theta_truth_C[, r]   y_obs_C <- forward_C(theta_t) + rnorm(n_obs_C, sd = 0.05)   weights_C <- rep(1 / 0.05, n_obs_C)   parcov_C  <- rep(1.0, n_par_C)    # Prior ensemble   par_C <- matrix(rnorm(n_real_C * n_par_C), n_par_C, n_real_C)   obs_C <- apply(par_C, 2L, forward_C)   phi0  <- mean(compute_phi(     matrix(rep(y_obs_C, n_real_C), nrow = n_obs_C) - obs_C, weights_C))    # Two IES iterations using ensemble_solution_gpu (adaptive backend) +   # ensemble_solution (kernel). Sign convention: obs_r = sim - obs.   par_d <- par_C - rowMeans(par_C); obs_d <- obs_C - rowMeans(obs_C)   obs_r <- obs_C - matrix(rep(y_obs_C, n_real_C), nrow = n_obs_C)   Am_C  <- matrix(rnorm(n_par_C * (n_real_C - 1L)), n_par_C, n_real_C - 1L)   gpu_res <- ensemble_solution_gpu(par_d, obs_d, obs_r, par_d,                                    weights_C, parcov_C, Am_C,                                    cur_lam = 1.0, svd_method = "auto")   gpu_times_ms[r] <- gpu_res$total_time_ms   par_C <- pmin(pmax(par_C + t(gpu_res$upgrade), -5), 5)   obs_C <- apply(par_C, 2L, forward_C)   par_d <- par_C - rowMeans(par_C); obs_d <- obs_C - rowMeans(obs_C)   obs_r <- obs_C - matrix(rep(y_obs_C, n_real_C), nrow = n_obs_C)   Am_C  <- matrix(rnorm(n_par_C * (n_real_C - 1L)), n_par_C, n_real_C - 1L)   upg2  <- ensemble_solution(par_d, obs_d, obs_r, par_d,                              weights_C, parcov_C, Am_C, cur_lam = 1.0)   par_C <- pmin(pmax(par_C + t(upg2), -5), 5)   obs_C <- apply(par_C, 2L, forward_C)   phi1 <- mean(compute_phi(     matrix(rep(y_obs_C, n_real_C), nrow = n_obs_C) - obs_C, weights_C))   phi_drop_rates[r] <- phi0 / phi1    # Adaptive ensemble size every replicate   phi_per_real <- compute_phi(     matrix(rep(y_obs_C, n_real_C), nrow = n_obs_C) - obs_C, weights_C)   asz <- adaptive_ensemble_size(phi_per_real, current_size = n_real_C)   adaptive_sizes[r] <- asz$recommended_size   ess_seq[[r]] <- asz$ess    # Surrogate-accelerated update (covers train_gp, predict_gp,   # surrogate_ensemble_update under the hood)   surr <- surrogate_ensemble_update(     par_ensemble = t(par_C), obs_ensemble = t(obs_C),     obs_target = y_obs_C,     weights = weights_C, parcov_inv = parcov_C,     uncertainty_threshold = 0.1   )   sur_savings[r] <- surr$savings_pct    # Sensitivity ranking via column-wise variance of obs_ens contributions   sens_score <- vapply(seq_len(n_par_C), function(k) {     var(obs_C[1, ]) * abs(cor(par_C[k, ], obs_C[1, ]))   }, numeric(1))   sens_rank[r, ] <- order(sens_score, decreasing = TRUE) }  # RFF surrogate one-shot to exercise train_rff_surrogate / predict_rff_surrogate set.seed(20260425L) X_rff <- t(par_C); Y_rff <- t(obs_C) rff_mod <- train_rff_surrogate(X_rff, Y_rff, n_features = 100L) rff_pred <- predict_rff_surrogate(rff_mod, X_rff) gp_mod   <- train_gp_surrogate(X_rff, Y_rff) gp_pred  <- predict_gp_surrogate(gp_mod, X_rff) cat(sprintf("RFF train MSE %.4g; RFF pred RMSE %.4f; GP pred RMSE %.4g\n",             rff_mod$train_mse,             sqrt(mean((rff_pred$mean - Y_rff)^2)),             sqrt(mean((gp_pred$mean  - Y_rff)^2)))) #> RFF train MSE 1.325e-08; RFF pred RMSE 0.0001; GP pred RMSE 0.1003

code: sim_summary <- data.table(   Metric = c("Median phi reduction (1 iter)",              "Mean surrogate savings (%)",              "Mean adaptive size",              "Median GPU-path time (ms)",              "Replicates"),   Value = c(round(median(phi_drop_rates), 2),             round(mean(sur_savings), 1),             round(mean(adaptive_sizes), 1),             round(median(gpu_times_ms), 2),             n_rep_C) ) knitr::kable(sim_summary, caption = "Scenario C: aggregate diagnostics.") ggplot(data.table(rate = phi_drop_rates), aes(x = rate)) +   geom_histogram(bins = 16L, fill = PAL[1], alpha = 0.8) +   scale_x_log10() +   labs(title = "Scenario C: distribution of phi reduction (one IES iteration)",        x = expression(Phi[0] / Phi[1] ~ "(log scale)"),        y = "Replicates") +   theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +   theme(plot.title = element_text(face = "bold")) ess_dt <- data.table(replicate = seq_len(n_rep_C),                      savings_pct = sur_savings,                      adaptive_size = adaptive_sizes,                      phi_drop = phi_drop_rates) ggplot(ess_dt, aes(x = adaptive_size, y = savings_pct, colour = log10(phi_drop))) +   geom_point(size = 2.6, alpha = 0.85) +   scale_colour_viridis_c(option = "C", name = expression(log[10](Phi[0]/Phi[1]))) +   labs(title = "Surrogate savings vs adaptive ensemble size",        x = "Recommended ensemble size",        y = "Surrogate evaluations saved (%)") +   theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +   theme(plot.title = element_text(face = "bold"),         legend.position = "right")

title: Surrogate-Accelerated Iterative Ensemble Smoother
text: surrogate-accelerated IES PESTO’s approach reducing number expensive forward model evaluations iterative parameter estimation. embeds online Gaussian Process (GP) surrogate within IES update loop, using uncertainty-driven switching decide ensemble members require full model evaluation can use cheaper surrogate prediction. vignette demonstrates full workflow nonlinear test problem.

title: Surrogate-Accelerated Iterative Ensemble Smoother
text: Gaussian-process surrogate inside PESTO tool clearly bounded operating envelope, worth stating envelope demo. empirical soft floor recommend n_train >= 5 * n_params – ratio GP posterior variance rarely drops far enough uncertainty-driven switching rule fire, surrogate honestly defers full model realisations. second axis smoothness forward model: smooth, near-linear responses well-approximated RBF-kernel GP yield large savings, whereas rough near-discontinuous responses (sharp wetting fronts, threshold activations, regime switches) defeat kernel savings collapse. third axis ensemble size – larger ensembles improve GP fit roughly 𝒪(n−1)\mathcal{O}(n^{-1}) posterior variance, ensembles fewer twenty realisations rarely produce usable surrogate dimensionality. problem falls outside envelope (high n_params / n_train ratio, rough forward model, tiny ensemble), right thing run pure IES via ensemble_solution() – surrogate_ensemble_update() report near-zero savings rather degrading posterior, pay GP training cost return.

title: Surrogate-Accelerated Iterative Ensemble Smoother
text: define nonlinear forward model inspired groundwater flow, response includes linear sensitivity nonlinear interaction term: yj=∑kGjkθk+αsin⁡(∑kGjkθk)exp⁡(−0.1|∑kGjkθk|)+εjy_j = \sum_k G_{jk}\theta_k + \alpha \sin\!\left(\sum_k G_{jk}\theta_k\right) \exp\!\left(-0.1\left|\sum_k G_{jk}\theta_k\right|\right) + \varepsilon_j parameter α\alpha controls nonlinearity: α=0\alpha = 0 linear, α=1\alpha = 1 strongly nonlinear.
code: library(PESTO) library(data.table) #>  #> Attaching package: 'data.table' #> The following object is masked from 'package:base': #>  #>     %notin% library(ggplot2)  forward_model <- function(theta, G, alpha = 0.3) {   linear <- as.numeric(G %*% theta)   nonlinear <- alpha * sin(linear) * exp(-0.1 * abs(linear))   linear + nonlinear }  set.seed(42) n_par <- 20 n_obs <- 50 n_real <- 50  theta_true <- rnorm(n_par) G <- matrix(rnorm(n_obs * n_par, sd = 1 / sqrt(n_par)), n_obs, n_par) y_obs <- forward_model(theta_true, G, alpha = 0.3) + rnorm(n_obs, sd = 0.1)

title: Surrogate-Accelerated Iterative Ensemble Smoother
code: par_ens <- matrix(rnorm(n_real * n_par, sd = 1.5), n_real, n_par)  # Run forward model for each realisation obs_ens <- t(apply(par_ens, 1, function(p) {   forward_model(p, G, alpha = 0.3) + rnorm(n_obs, sd = 0.1) }))  cat("Parameter ensemble:", nrow(par_ens), "realisations x", ncol(par_ens), "parameters\n") #> Parameter ensemble: 50 realisations x 20 parameters cat("Observation ensemble:", nrow(obs_ens), "realisations x", ncol(obs_ens), "observations\n") #> Observation ensemble: 50 realisations x 50 observations

title: Surrogate-Accelerated Iterative Ensemble Smoother
text: surrogate_ensemble_update() function performs full algorithm: Train GP current ensemble Predict model outputs realisations Classify uncertainty: high → full model, low → surrogate Apply control-variate bias correction Compute IES update blended ensemble

weights <- rep(1 / 0.1, n_obs)    # 1 / obs_noise parcov_inv <- rep(1 / 1.5^2, n_par)  # 1 / prior_var  result <- surrogate_ensemble_update(   par_ensemble = par_ens,   obs_ensemble = obs_ens,   obs_target = y_obs,   weights = weights,   parcov_inv = parcov_inv,   cur_lam = 1.0,   uncertainty_threshold = 0.1 )  cat("Full model runs needed:", result$n_model_runs, "
") #> Full model runs needed: 0 cat("Surrogate predictions:", result$n_surrogate_runs, "
") #> Surrogate predictions: 50 cat("Total realisations:", result$n_total, "
") #> Total realisations: 50 cat("Model savings:", sprintf("%.0f%%", result$savings_pct), "
") #> Model savings: 100% cat("Mean GP uncertainty:", round(result$mean_uncertainty, 6), "

title: Surrogate-Accelerated Iterative Ensemble Smoother
code: # Standard IES update (for comparison) par_mean <- colMeans(par_ens) obs_mean <- colMeans(obs_ens) par_diff <- t(par_ens) - par_mean obs_diff <- t(obs_ens) - obs_mean # Sign: ensemble_solution() expects sim - obs (see ?ensemble_solution). obs_resid <- t(obs_ens) - matrix(rep(y_obs, n_real), n_obs, n_real) par_resid <- par_diff Am <- matrix(rnorm(n_par * (n_real - 1)), n_par, n_real - 1)  standard_upgrade <- ensemble_solution(   par_diff, obs_diff, obs_resid, par_resid,   weights, parcov_inv, Am, cur_lam = 1.0 )  surrogate_upgrade <- result$upgrade  # Compare upgrades upgrade_diff <- sqrt(mean((standard_upgrade - surrogate_upgrade)^2)) upgrade_corr <- cor(as.numeric(standard_upgrade), as.numeric(surrogate_upgrade))  cat("Upgrade RMSE difference:", round(upgrade_diff, 6), "\n") #> Upgrade RMSE difference: 0.000118 cat("Upgrade correlation:", round(upgrade_corr, 6), "\n") #> Upgrade correlation: 1

title: Surrogate-Accelerated Iterative Ensemble Smoother
text: surrogate accuracy vary model nonlinearity? Surrogate RMSE ratio across nonlinearity levels.
code: results_dt <- rbindlist(lapply(seq(0, 1, by = 0.1), function(alpha) {   set.seed(42)   y_a <- forward_model(theta_true, G, alpha) + rnorm(n_obs, sd = 0.1)   obs_a <- t(apply(par_ens, 1, function(p) {     forward_model(p, G, alpha) + rnorm(n_obs, sd = 0.1)   }))    pm <- colMeans(par_ens); om <- colMeans(obs_a)   pd <- t(par_ens) - pm; od <- t(obs_a) - om   or_ <- t(obs_a) - matrix(rep(y_a, n_real), n_obs, n_real)   pr <- pd   Am_a <- matrix(rnorm(n_par * (n_real - 1)), n_par, n_real - 1)    upg_std <- ensemble_solution(pd, od, or_, pr, weights, parcov_inv, Am_a, 1.0)   par_std <- par_ens + upg_std   rmse_std <- sqrt(mean((colMeans(par_std) - theta_true)^2))    surr <- surrogate_ensemble_update(par_ens, obs_a, y_a, weights, parcov_inv,                                      uncertainty_threshold = 0.1)   par_surr <- par_ens + surr$upgrade   rmse_surr <- sqrt(mean((colMeans(par_surr) - theta_true)^2))    data.table(alpha = alpha, rmse_std = rmse_std, rmse_surr = rmse_surr,              ratio = rmse_surr / rmse_std, savings = surr$savings_pct) }))  ggplot(results_dt, aes(x = alpha)) +   geom_line(aes(y = ratio), colour = "#009E73", linewidth = 1.2) +   geom_point(aes(y = ratio), colour = "#009E73", size = 3) +   geom_hline(yintercept = 1.0, linetype = "dashed", colour = "grey50") +   scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0.95, 1.05)) +   labs(title = "Surrogate Accuracy Across Nonlinearity Levels",        x = expression(paste("Nonlinearity strength ", alpha)),        y = "RMSE ratio (surrogate / standard)",        caption = "Ratio = 1.0 means identical posterior quality") +   theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +   theme(plot.title = element_text(face = "bold"))

what: PESTO: Parameter Estimation, Surrogates, and Tooling for Optimisation — PESTO-package
title: PESTO: Parameter Estimation, Surrogates, and Tooling for Optimisation — PESTO-package
text: High-performance parameter estimation, uncertainty quantification, inverse modelling toolkit built modernised PEST++ algorithms. Provides native R interfaces iterative ensemble smoothers (IES), Gauss-Levenberg-Marquardt (GLM) solvers, global sensitivity analysis, multi-objective optimisation uncertainty. Includes surrogate-accelerated IES via Gaussian Process Random Fourier Features, adaptive SVD backends (randomised SVD, LAPACK, Eigen), convergence-aware adaptive ensemble sizing. Designed large-scale environmental, hydrological, agricultural models support highly-parameterised problems (>100,000 parameters).

title: Ensemble Solution Kernel (GLM form) — ensemble_solution
text: Implements core IES ensemble update equation using Gauss-Levenberg-Marquardt (GLM) formulation Chen & Oliver (2013). computational hotspot iterative ensemble smoother.

title: Ensemble Solution Kernel (GLM form) — ensemble_solution
code: ensemble_solution(   par_diff,   obs_diff,   obs_resid,   par_resid,   weights,   parcov_inv,   Am,   cur_lam,   eigthresh = 1e-06,   use_approx = TRUE,   use_prior_scaling = FALSE,   iter = 1L,   reg_factor = -1 )

title: Ensemble Solution Kernel (GLM form) — ensemble_solution
text: par_diff Matrix (npar x nreal). Parameter anomalies (deviations mean). obs_diff Matrix (nobs x nreal). Observation anomalies. obs_resid Matrix (nobs x nreal). Observation residuals, simulated minus observed (sim - obs). sign convention required leading negative GLM update (\(\Delta\theta = -\Delta\theta' V s (s^2 + \lambda )^{-1} U^T r\)) yields descent step \(\Phi = \|W(g(\theta) - d^{obs})\|^2\). Passing (obs - sim) inverts gradient causes phi diverge. par_resid Matrix (npar x nreal). Parameter residuals (par - prior mean). weights Numeric vector (nobs). Observation weights (1/sqrt(variance)). parcov_inv Numeric vector (npar). Diagonal inverse parameter covariance. Matrix (npar x nreal-1). Random matrix upgrade_2 (optional). cur_lam Numeric. Current Marquardt lambda. eigthresh Numeric. Eigenvalue truncation threshold (0-1). use_approx Logical. TRUE, skip upgrade_2 (prior-scaling correction). use_prior_scaling Logical. Scale prior covariance. iter Integer. Current iteration number. reg_factor Numeric. Regularisation factor upgrade_2 blending.

title: Ensemble Solution Kernel (GLM form) — ensemble_solution
text: Matrix (nreal x npar). Parameter upgrade vectors (one row per realisation). returned matrix negative-direction step Chen-Oliver 2013 GLM update formula. advance ensemble, apply subtraction: par_new = par_old - upgrade. R-side driver pesto_ies_callback() handles convention internally.

title: Ensemble Solution Kernel (GLM form) — ensemble_solution
text: update equation solves: $$\Delta\theta = -\Delta\theta' V s (s^2 + \lambda )^{-1} U^T r$$ SVD performed scaled observation difference matrix.

title: Ensemble Solution Kernel (GLM form) — ensemble_solution
code: set.seed(1L) npar  <- 4L nreal <- 20L nobs  <- 30L par_diff  <- matrix(rnorm(npar * nreal), npar, nreal) obs_diff  <- matrix(rnorm(nobs * nreal), nobs, nreal) obs_resid <- matrix(rnorm(nobs * nreal, sd = 0.5), nobs, nreal) par_resid <- matrix(rnorm(npar * nreal, sd = 0.1), npar, nreal) weights    <- rep(1, nobs) parcov_inv <- rep(1, npar) Am         <- matrix(0, 0, 0) upgrade <- ensemble_solution(   par_diff   = par_diff,   obs_diff   = obs_diff,   obs_resid  = obs_resid,   par_resid  = par_resid,   weights    = weights,   parcov_inv = parcov_inv,   Am         = Am,   cur_lam    = 1.0 ) dim(upgrade) #> [1] 20  4

title: PESTO Ensemble Manifest (S7 class) — pesto_ensemble_manifest
text: See write_manifest() / read_manifest() YAML serialisation verify_manifest() integrity checking. YAML file authoritative human-readable entry; params, outputs, assimilation context (weights + obs_target) emitted sidecar files whose relative paths recorded inside YAML. Three sidecar modes: format = "rds" (default, verifiable) — RDS . IEEE 754 doubles round-trip bit-exactly; SHA-256 integrity check holds. format = "" (verifiable) — RDS sidecars plus parallel *_inspection.csv files. Hash bound RDS; CSVs decorative. format = "csv_unverified" (verifiable, renamed "csv" PESTO 0.3.2) — CSV-sidecars. Hash recorded recomputed disk (CSV formatter precision loss ~1 ULP). verify_manifest() returns ok = NA + message. Use one-way export non-R analysts; round-trip integrity construction broken. YAML carries explicit integrity: field ("verifiable" / "not_verifiable") derived format downstream non-R tools can branch integrity contract without needing know kernR/PESTO-specific format vocabulary.

title: PESTO Ensemble Manifest (S7 class) — pesto_ensemble_manifest
code: set.seed(1) npar <- 2L; nobs <- 3L; nreal <- 30L G <- matrix(stats::rnorm(nobs * npar), nobs, npar) y <- as.numeric(G %*% c(0.5, -1.0)) + stats::rnorm(nobs, sd = 0.05) names(y) <- paste0("o", seq_len(nobs)) prior <- matrix(stats::rnorm(nreal * npar), nreal, npar,                 dimnames = list(NULL, c("a", "b"))) fit <- pesto_ies_callback(function(t) t %*% t(G), prior, y, 0.05,                           noptmax = 3, verbose = FALSE) m <- as_manifest(fit, seed = 1L) verify_manifest(m)$ok #> [1] TRUE

title: Run IES with an In-Process R Callback Forward Model — pesto_ies_callback
text: forward_model function signature function(theta) -> obs, theta nreal x npar numeric matrix obs nreal x nobs numeric matrix. Failed realisations may return rows NA; driver tolerates (see on_failure). prior_ensemble Matrix data.table, nreal x npar. Columns parameters; optional real_name column preserved present. Column names supply parameter names. obs Named numeric vector. Target observations. obs_sd Numeric scalar vector length nobs. Observation standard deviation(s); IES weights 1/obs_sd. noptmax Integer. Number IES iterations (default 4). lambda Numeric scalar vector. Marquardt lambda per iteration. scalar recycled; vector shorter noptmax right-padded last value (default 1.0). parcov Numeric vector length npar, diagonal prior parameter covariance. Defaults column-wise variance prior_ensemble; zero negative entries replaced 1.0. eigthresh Numeric. SVD eigenvalue truncation threshold (default 1e-6). use_approx Logical. TRUE (default), skip prior-scaling correction (upgrade_2); matches typical pestpp-ies default. on_failure Character. "na" (default) carries failed realisations forward unchanged proceeds; "stop" aborts failure. verbose Logical. Print per-iteration phi summaries.

title: Run IES with an In-Process R Callback Forward Model — pesto_ies_callback
text: list class c("pesto_ies_callback_result", "pesto_ies_result") components: phi data.table per-realisation phi iteration. par_ensemble Final parameter ensemble (data.table). obs_ensemble Final simulated-observation ensemble (data.table). iterations List per-iteration metadata (lambda, mean phi, failure count). runtime_seconds Total wall-clock runtime. n_forward_evals Total number realisation-level forward evaluations across iterations (including final refresh). failure_rate Fraction forward evaluations returned NA.

title: Run IES with an In-Process R Callback Forward Model — pesto_ies_callback
text: Evaluates forward_model(par_ensemble) obtain simulated observations. Forms parameter / observation anomalies residuals. Calls C++ kernel ensemble_solution() (GLM form, Chen & Oliver 2013) compute nreal x npar upgrade. Adds upgrade current ensemble. classic .pst-file path remains available via pesto_ies() full PEST++ compatibility. Use callback driver forward model R function (e.g. apsimx wrapper apsim_callback(), Python bridge, synthetic test problem) per-realisation file-/O overhead .pst path bottleneck. Phase-1 behaviour: single lambda per iteration (user-supplied schedule). line-search ies_lambda_mults matching pestpp-ies planned Phase-2 enhancement; common case well-behaved forward model lambda = 1, GLM update reduces phi reliably (see vignette apsim-callback).

title: Run IES with an In-Process R Callback Forward Model — pesto_ies_callback
text: Chen, Y. & Oliver, D.S. (2013). Levenberg-Marquardt forms iterative ensemble smoother efficient history matching uncertainty quantification. Computational Geosciences, 17(4), 689–703.

title: Run IES with an In-Process R Callback Forward Model — pesto_ies_callback
code: # Linear-Gaussian recovery toy set.seed(1) npar <- 3; nobs <- 6; nreal <- 80 G <- matrix(rnorm(nobs * npar), nobs, npar) theta_true <- c(1.0, -0.5, 2.0) y <- as.numeric(G %*% theta_true) + rnorm(nobs, sd = 0.05) f <- function(theta) theta %*% t(G) prior <- matrix(rnorm(nreal * npar), nreal, npar,                 dimnames = list(NULL, paste0("p", 1:npar))) fit <- pesto_ies_callback(   forward_model = f, prior_ensemble = prior,   obs = setNames(y, paste0("o", 1:nobs)), obs_sd = 0.05,   noptmax = 5, verbose = FALSE ) colMeans(as.matrix(fit$par_ensemble[, -1]))  # should approach theta_true #>         p1         p2         p3  #>  1.0136905 -0.4936753  1.9847267

title: Pure-R Reference IES Update — Chen & Oliver (2013) eq. 12 — pesto_reference_ies
text: Independent numerical validation. PESTO's C++ kernel can cross-checked reference machine precision (package's regression test tests/testthat/test-ensemble-solution-sign.R paired-seed protocol inst/scripts/i2_paired_seed_check.R rely ). Self-contained pestpp-ies comparison. Vignettes can compare PESTO native IES textbook reference without requiring upstream pestpp-ies binary, making comparison story reproducible CRAN check farms binary-free environments. implementation deliberately pedagogical — readability speed. production work use ensemble_solution() (C++ kernel; \(\sim 35\times\) faster production-scale ensembles).

title: Pure-R Reference IES Update — Chen & Oliver (2013) eq. 12 — pesto_reference_ies
text: reference uses textbook sign obs_resid = obs - sim positive leading sign upgrade. PESTO's C++ kernel uses equivalent obs_resid = sim - obs leading negative sign (see ?ensemble_solution). yield identical upgrades; difference purely cosmetic. two cross-validated machine precision inst/scripts/i2_paired_seed_check.R.

title: Pure-R Reference IES Update — Chen & Oliver (2013) eq. 12 — pesto_reference_ies
text: Chen, Y. & Oliver, D. S. (2013). Levenberg-Marquardt forms iterative ensemble smoother efficient history matching uncertainty quantification. Computational Geosciences, 17(4), 689–703. doi:10.1007/s10596-013-9351-5 Evensen, G. (2018). Analysis iterative ensemble smoothers solving inverse problems. Computational Geosciences, 22(3), 885–908. doi:10.1007/s10596-018-9731-y

title: Pure-R Reference IES Update — Chen & Oliver (2013) eq. 12 — pesto_reference_ies
code: # Simple linear inverse problem: identify the true theta given noisy obs. set.seed(20260425L) n_par <- 4L; n_obs <- 12L; n_real <- 30L G <- matrix(rnorm(n_obs * n_par) / sqrt(n_par), n_obs, n_par) theta_true <- rnorm(n_par) y_obs <- as.numeric(G %*% theta_true) + rnorm(n_obs, sd = 0.05) weights <- rep(1 / 0.05, n_obs)  par_ens <- matrix(rnorm(n_par * n_real, sd = 1), n_par, n_real) obs_ens <- G %*% par_ens  # One textbook IES upgrade. upg <- pesto_reference_ies(   par_ensemble = par_ens,   obs_ensemble = obs_ens,   obs_target   = y_obs,   weights      = weights,   lambda       = 1.0 ) dim(upg) #> [1]  4 30  # Apply the upgrade and check phi reduces. par_next <- par_ens + upg obs_next <- G %*% par_next Y <- matrix(rep(y_obs, n_real), n_obs, n_real) phi0 <- mean(compute_phi(Y - obs_ens,  weights)) phi1 <- mean(compute_phi(Y - obs_next, weights)) phi1 < phi0 #> [1] TRUE

Target observations. weights Numeric vector (nobs). Observation weights. parcov_inv Numeric vector (npar). Inverse parameter covariance diagonal. cur_lam Numeric. title: Surrogate-Accelerated Ensemble Update — surrogate_ensemble_update
text: Algorithm: Train GP surrogate current ensemble evaluations Generate candidate upgrades using surrogate predictions Evaluate uncertainty surrogate predictions Run full model realisations uncertainty exceeds threshold Blend surrogate model results using control-variate correction typically reduces full model evaluations 50-90%.

title: Surrogate-Accelerated Ensemble Update — surrogate_ensemble_update
code: # \donttest{ set.seed(1L) npar  <- 5L nreal <- 15L nobs  <- 8L par_ensemble <- matrix(rnorm(nreal * npar), nreal, npar) obs_ensemble <- matrix(rnorm(nreal * nobs), nreal, nobs) obs_target   <- rnorm(nobs) weights      <- rep(1, nobs) parcov_inv   <- rep(1, npar) res <- surrogate_ensemble_update(   par_ensemble = par_ensemble,   obs_ensemble = obs_ensemble,   obs_target   = obs_target,   weights      = weights,   parcov_inv   = parcov_inv,   cur_lam      = 1.0 ) dim(res$upgrade) #> [1] 15  5 res$savings_pct #> [1] 100 # }

title: Train a Sparse GP Surrogate via Random Fourier Features — train_rff_surrogate
text: RBF kernel k(x,x') = sigma^2 exp(-||x-x'||^2 / 2l^2) approximated k(x,x') ~ z(x)^T z(x') z(x) = sqrt(2/D) * cos(Wx + b), W random frequencies. w_j ~ N(0, /l^2) b_j ~ Uniform(0, 2pi).

title: Write a manifest to YAML + sidecar data files — write_manifest
text: "rds" (default) — RDS sidecars . IEEE 754 doubles round-trip bit-exactly; verify_manifest() recomputes SHA-256 hash confirms integrity. "" — RDS sidecars plus parallel inspection CSVs (<basename>_params_inspection.csv, etc.). hash still bound RDS form; CSVs decorative recorded YAML's inspection_csv: block. "csv_unverified" — CSV sidecars . hash still recorded (computed -memory binary representation) verify_manifest() recompute disk: CSV write-formatter precision loss (~1 ULP IEEE 754 epsilon) falsely fail check. YAML carries integrity: not_verifiable downstream tools can branch accordingly. Renamed "csv" PESTO 0.3.2 (post critical review): old name indistinguishable glance verifiable modes, review judged footgun. Old YAMLs format: csv continue read back correctly.

title: PESTO 0.3.1
text: write_manifest() gains format = c("rds", "", "csv") argument. "rds" (default) preserves current bit-exact binary behaviour. "" writes RDS sidecars plus parallel CSV inspection files (*_inspection.csv); SHA-256 hash stays bound RDS. "csv" writes CSV-sidecars inspection / interchange workflows bit-exact integrity required. New S7 slot format pesto_ensemble_manifest records -disk serialisation mode (default "rds"; preserved read/write round-trips). Validator enforces three-value vocabulary. read_manifest() dispatches file extension YAML’s artefacts: block — reads RDS via readRDS(), CSV via utils::read.csv(). verify_manifest() gains message field return list returns ok = NA (explanation) format = "csv" manifests whose IEEE 754 doubles round-tripped write formatter. Existing format = "rds" callers see behaviour change; new field NULL case. YAML schema picks top-level format: key optional inspection_csv: block format = "". Backwards-compatible: YAMLs written PESTO 0.3.0 (format: key) read back format = "rds" per default. schema-version bump required. New tests test-manifest.R cover three formats plus unknown-format rejection path.

what: Ensemble Manifest as S7 Cross-Package Contract (roadmap §A5)
title: PESTO 0.3.0
text: New S7 class pesto_ensemble_manifest — versioned, hashed, provenance-tracked container ensemble-run output. Slots cover params, outputs, weights, obs_target, seed, data_hash (SHA-256), fidelity, apsim_version, pesto_version, timestamp, plus method context (method, noptmax, lambda_schedule, failure_rate). contract object downstream consumers (kernR, proxymix, paper-skill) read. New as_manifest() — S7 generic method pesto_ies_callback_result. Non-destructive: wraps without mutating source result. New write_manifest() / read_manifest() — YAML+RDS serialisation. YAML carries metadata + relative paths three sidecar RDS files (*_params.rds, *_outputs.rds, *_assim.rds); RDS used preference CSV IEEE 754 doubles round-trip bit-exactly (SHA-256 integrity check otherwise trip CSV formatter precision loss). New verify_manifest() — recomputes SHA-256 (params, outputs, weights, obs_target, seed) compares stored value, returning diagnostic list. Detects post-write tampering sidecar CSVs. New vignette: ensemble-manifest — end--end demo construct → write → read → verify, plus tamper-detection.

what: In-Process IES via R Callback (UQ ag-stack roadmap §D4)
title: PESTO 0.3.0
text: New pesto_ies_callback() — drives Iterative Ensemble Smoother entirely R using user-supplied forward-model callable, bypassing .pst-file write/read cycle pesto_ies(). iteration calls existing C++ kernel ensemble_solution() (Chen & Oliver, 2013). Tolerates per-realisation failures via on_failure = c("na", "stop") reports failure_rate result object. Phase-1 behaviour uses single lambda per iteration (user-supplied schedule); full pestpp-ies-style lambda line-search planned Phase-2 enhancement. New apsim_callback() — adapter wraps apsimx package (now Suggests) forward-model closure suitable pesto_ies_callback().
